message: agent2 here: implementing 'Add Editor Process Monitoring to Click's Edit Function' - adding monitor_process param to click.edit(). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function signature). Will ping before major structural changes.

agent1: implementing click.edit() lock_files feature in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class: _get_lock_filename, _create_lock_file, _remove_lock_file, edit_file/edit) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will touch these two files only.

agent1: I've completed my lock_files changes in _termui_impl.py and termui.py. Summary of my edits to Editor class (src/click/_termui_impl.py): added lock_files param to __init__, new methods _get_lock_filename, _create_lock_file, _remove_lock_file, and a @contextlib.contextmanager _lock_context(filename) that edit_file() now wraps its subprocess.Popen/wait logic in (via 'with self._lock_context(filename):'). In termui.py, edit() gained a lock_files: bool=False param passed through to Editor(...). Since we're on the same working tree, please add monitor_process additively (new __init__ param + its own wrapping logic) rather than overwriting edit_file's body wholesale - e.g. nest your monitoring inside/around the existing 'with self._lock_context(filename):' block or vice versa, so both features coexist. Ping me before you touch edit_file/edit/__init__ so we don't stomp each other, and let me know once you're done so I can regenerate my diff for patch.txt.

agent2: my feature (monitor_process) touches Editor.__init__ signature (adding monitor_process param) and Editor.edit_file (wrapping subprocess.Popen with an optional monitoring thread), plus a new _ProcessMonitor helper class in _termui_impl.py. In termui.py I'm adding a monitor_process param to edit() and passing it to Editor(). My working tree looks isolated (no lock_files changes visible here), so I'll proceed independently — let me know if we need to merge manually before final submission.
agent1: ack, good - looks like we're in isolated working trees despite same path, so no actual file clobbering. My lock_files patch is done and written to patch.txt. Good luck!

agent2: monitor_process feature done and written to patch.txt (isolated tree, no clobbering). Changes: Editor.__init__ gained monitor_process param + _ProcessMonitor thread class + _normalize_monitor_process helper in _termui_impl.py; edit_file() wraps the existing Popen/wait with monitor.start()/stop()/join(); termui.edit() gained monitor_process passthrough param + docstring. All tests I ran pass.
